symptomSummary: After a prompt revision accumulates ~30+ invocations, subsequent evolve calls produce revisions that address old feedback rather than recent feedback, causing quality to stagnate or regress.
rootCauseHypothesis: The feedback retrieval used by the evolve step fetches all historical feedback for a revision without a recency window or LIMIT, so as invocation count grows the older (majority) feedback items dominate the set passed to the evolution agent. Because older failures outnumber newer ones, the LLM optimises for problems that may already be resolved.
fixHypothesis: The feedback query or aggregation in the evolution service (or the repository it calls) needs a sliding-window constraint — either ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT N in the SQL query, or a post-fetch filter that discards feedback older than the most recent N events — so only fresh signal reaches the evolution step regardless of total invocation count.

primaryLocation.rationale: This file is the dedicated service for prompt evolution; it is almost certainly the code path that collects feedback records and forwards them to the enhancer/LLM. A threshold-shaped regression (works fine at low invocation counts, breaks at ~30) is the classic signature of an unbounded fetch that grows until older items dominate. The service layer is the natural place where 'get all feedback for revision X' becomes 'get recent feedback for revision X'.
primaryLocation.function: evolve (or equivalent method that gathers feedback before calling the LLM)

candidateLocations.rationale: If the evolution service delegates to a repository to fetch feedback, the missing LIMIT / ORDER BY created_at DESC clause lives here. Fixing the query at this layer would be the cleanest fix and would prevent the same problem in any other caller.

If the evolution service passes raw feedback through to the enhancer agent, the filtering/windowing logic might need to live here before the feedback array is serialised into the prompt context. The large file size suggests it may own feedback pre-processing.
candidateLocations.function: enhance or equivalent agent entry point

candidateLocations.rationale: The API route is the entry point for evolve calls; it may assemble the feedback payload itself before handing off to the service. If feedback is collected and sliced here rather than in the service, the recency window fix belongs at this layer.

Client-side UI component that drives evolution; if it passes pre-fetched feedback to the API it could be sending an unbounded list. Less likely than the server-side paths but worth checking if client controls the feedback payload size.
